The first target resistance level was 125,000, while the actual top came in at 124,517. In practice, profit-taking should typically occur within about 1–3% of the target zone, rather than waiting for the exact level to be tagged.

The first target resistance level was 250. Price reached that level on Sep 18, and then closed below the 21EMA support on Sep 22. These two levels are taken together as the average exit price for the trade
The exit price is calculated as the average of the key resistance levels, including the overextension that occurred on Oct 3.
The original full risk level was lowered to the prior local high (from 1.51 to 1.38) on Nov 11, the day price started to follow through to the downside.
The exit price is calculated as the average of two target support levels reached on Nov 17 and Nov 21 (see chart updates).
Full stop / risk is adjusted to the set-up outlined on April 1
added on Apr 16 with staggered stop at 83 and 81.35
